name: Uruguay

visa_free.notes: Visa-free entry. 90-day stay, extendable for additional 90 days. Mercosur member.
Visa-free entry. 90-day stay. Mercosur associate member.
Visa-free entry. 90-day stay. Mercosur associate member.
Visa-free entry. 90-day stay. Mercosur associate member.
Visa-free entry. 90-day stay. Mercosur associate member.
Visa-free for tourist purposes (Note 6). When travel purpose is non-tourism, labor-related, or extending stay beyond 90 days, prior consultation required.

visa_free.nationality: Hong Kong SAR (HKSAR passport holders only)
visa_free.notes: Visa-free entry. 90-day stay. HKSAR passport only; not applicable to other Hong Kong travel documents.
Visa-free for tourist purposes. For non-tourism, labor-related, or stays exceeding 90 days, prior consultation is required (Note 6). EU member.
30-day temporary admission without visa (Admisión Temporaria Sin Visa, ATSV). Different from standard 90-day rule.

visa_free.nationality: Macao SAR (ordinary passports and Macao travel documents)
visa_free.notes: Visa-free entry. 90-day stay. Note 11: holders of ordinary passports and travel documents of the Macao Special Administrative Region (Macao RAE) are exempt from the visa requirement.
Visa-free entry. 90-day stay. Mercosur associate member.
Visa-free for tourist purposes (Note 6). When travel purpose is non-tourism, labor-related, or extending stay beyond 90 days, prior consultation required before entry.

visa_free.nationality: Kittitian and Nevisian (Saint Kitts and Nevis)

visa_free.notes: Visa-free for tourist purposes. When travel purpose is non-tourism, labor-related, or extending stay beyond 90 days, prior consultation required (Note 6).
Visa-free for tourist purposes. When travel purpose is non-tourism, labor-related, or extending stay beyond 90 days, prior consultation required (Note 6).
Visa-free for tourism (Note 6). If travel purpose is non-tourism, labor-related, or stay exceeds 90 days, prior consultation is required before entry.

Visa-free entry for ordinary and diplomatic passports. 90-day stay. Note: Andorra is an unusual case where ordinary and diplomatic passport holders are visa-free but service passport holders require a visa.

visa_free.nationality: Sovereign Military Order of Malta (SMOM passport holders)
visa_free.notes: Visa-free entry. 90-day stay. Applies to holders of SMOM (Ordine di Malta / Soberana Orden de Malta) passports; not a UN member state but issues travel documents recognized by Uruguay.

visa_policy.notes: The official visa regime (Régimen de Visas de Admisión) is published by Uruguay's Ministry of Interior (Dirección Nacional de Migración) at gub.uy. The default tourist visa-free stay is 90 days, extendable for an additional 90 days with multiple entries. Uruguay has no visa-on-arrival or independent e-visa portal; what MRREE launched in 2014 as 'visados electrónicos' is a consular electronic processing system (SIGMA), not an independent e-visa application system. All non-listed nationalities require a prior consular visa. The official source page is in Spanish; country names appear in Spanish in the original document. Dominican Republic common passport holders are exempt only when holding a valid US or UK visa with 6+ months remaining. Taiwan (Chinese Taipei) requires a visa (listed as 'Necesita Visa (8)' on official table). Hong Kong SAR exemption applies only to HKSAR passport holders (Note 4). Macao SAR ordinary passport and travel document holders are visa-free (Note 11). Note 6 applies to Dominica, Guyana, Latvia, Russia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, South Korea: visa-free for tourism only; non-tourism or stays exceeding 90 days require prior consular consultation.

conditional_access.conditions: Portadores de pasaporte común de República Popular China con visa vigente de EE.UU., Canadá, Reino Unido o UE pueden ingresar sin visa consular uruguaya siempre que el primer ingreso sea por Carrasco, Montevideo, Colonia o Laguna del Sauce. One of the four qualifying issuers (US, Canada, UK, or EU) suffices. First entry must be through Carrasco (Montevideo), Colonia, or Laguna del Sauce airports/ports.

Portadores de pasaporte común de República Popular China con visa vigente de EE.UU., Canadá, Reino Unido o UE pueden ingresar sin visa consular uruguaya siempre que el primer ingreso sea por Carrasco, Montevideo, Colonia o Laguna del Sauce. One of the four qualifying issuers (US, Canada, UK, or EU) suffices. First entry must be through Carrasco (Montevideo), Colonia, or Laguna del Sauce airports/ports.

Portadores de pasaporte común de República Popular China con visa vigente de EE.UU., Canadá, Reino Unido o UE pueden ingresar sin visa consular uruguaya siempre que el primer ingreso sea por Carrasco, Montevideo, Colonia o Laguna del Sauce. One of the four qualifying issuers (US, Canada, UK, or EU) suffices. First entry must be through Carrasco (Montevideo), Colonia, or Laguna del Sauce airports/ports.

Portadores de pasaporte común de República Popular China con visa vigente de EE.UU., Canadá, Reino Unido o UE pueden ingresar sin visa consular uruguaya siempre que el primer ingreso sea por Carrasco, Montevideo, Colonia o Laguna del Sauce. One of the four qualifying issuers (US, Canada, UK, or EU) suffices. First entry must be through Carrasco (Montevideo), Colonia, or Laguna del Sauce airports/ports.

Portadores de pasaporte común de República Dominicana con visa de EE.UU. o Reino Unido vigente por al menos 6 meses pueden ingresar sin visa consular estampada. Visa must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Either US or UK visa qualifies.

Portadores de pasaporte común de República Dominicana con visa de EE.UU. o Reino Unido vigente por al menos 6 meses pueden ingresar sin visa consular estampada. Visa must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Either US or UK visa qualifies.

Angola: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión of Uruguay's Dirección Nacional de Migración.

Azerbaijan: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Belarus: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Cambodia: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Cuba: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Egypt: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

India: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión. No OCI cardholder exemption found in official sources.

Indonesia: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Laos: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Morocco (Marruecos): ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Namibia: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Suriname: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Thailand (Tailandia): ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Tunisia (Túnez): ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

Vietnam: ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (Nota 5). Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

China (República Popular China): ordinary passport holders require a prior consular visa (with conditional exception for holders of valid US/Canada/UK/EU visa - see credential entries). Diplomatic passport holders only are visa-free for up to 30 days. Bilateral diplomatic passport agreement. Service/official passports not confirmed in this exemption.

Dominican Republic (República Dominicana): ordinary passport holders require a visa unless holding a valid US or UK visa with 6+ months remaining. Diplomatic and service/official passport holders are unconditionally visa-free. Bilateral exemption documented in the Régimen de Visas de Admisión.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program found on any official Uruguayan government source (gub.uy, uruguayxxi.gub.uy, mrree.gub.uy, minterior.gub.uy, vui.gub.uy, liveinuruguay.uy). Uruguay does not sell citizenship directly. The path to citizenship is through residency: after 3 years of legal residency, foreign nationals may apply for naturalization provided they can demonstrate non-criminal records, sufficient income, and settlement in the country.

programs.name: Legal Temporary Residency
programs.notes: Temporary residency for stays of more than 180 days and less than 2 years. Categories include workers/entrepreneurs (requires employment letter with contract details), students (enrollment proof and financial support), and religious (church credential and proof of livelihood). After 3 years of legal residency (cumulated), citizenship can be requested. No specific minimum investment amount required.

programs.name: Legal Permanent Residency (Non-Mercosur)
programs.notes: Permanent residency for non-Mercosur nationals intending to reside permanently in Uruguay. Must regularize situation through the National Department of Migration. No minimum investment required. After 3 years of legal residency, citizenship may be requested.

programs.name: Mercosur Permanent Residency
programs.notes: Granted to nationals born or naturalized in Mercosur member and associate states (Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, Venezuela, Bolivia, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Guyana, Suriname). Includes foreign family members of Uruguayan nationals. After 3 years of legal residency, citizenship may be requested.

programs.name: Mercosur Temporary Residency
programs.notes: Available to Mercosur and associated country nationals with 5+ years of citizenship. Duration: 2 years, renewable once for additional 2 years. Renewal must be requested 90 days before expiration. No minimum investment required.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Permit (TID-Nomads)
programs.notes: Established under Decree 238/022. Duration: 6 months, renewable for additional 6 months (12 months total). Eligibility: self-employed or employed by foreign companies. Must first enter as regular tourist. Requirements: online application form, affidavit of financial self-sufficiency, clean criminal record (for extension), vaccination certificate from Uruguay. No minimum income threshold specified on official page. Can transition to temporary or permanent residency. Contact: dnm-hip@minterior.gub.uy.

programs.name: Fast Track Residency
programs.eligibility: Investors, operators, technicians, managers, and middle management linked to exporting companies. Any foreigner hired by local or international exporting companies, whether currently in Uruguay or abroad.
programs.processing_time: Shortened timeframe vs standard; temporary residency duration: more than 6 months to less than 2 years.
programs.notes: Uruguay XXI facilitates expedited access to temporary residencies to attract talent. Employer must submit application to Uruguay XXI first, then individual initiates online residency process and pays fees, then appointment is scheduled at National Immigration Directorate. Two tracks: MERCOSUR countries (Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, Venezuela, Bolivia, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Peru, Suriname) and non-MERCOSUR countries. Provisional identity card can be obtained in 20 business days, with same working rights as nationals. Contact: fast-track@uruguayxxi.gub.uy.

agreements: Mercosur (founding member: Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, Venezuela; associated: Bolivia, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Peru, Suriname)

validation_notes: 2026-06-14 re-validation against official gub.uy/ministerio-interior/comunicacion/publicaciones/regimen-visas-admision (page dated 19/08/2025).

ADDED Andorra (AND) to visa_free: confirmed as 'No necesita Visa' (no condition note) for ordinary passports on official table. Was missing from prior record. Unusual: diplomatic and service passports for Andorra require a visa.
ADDED Liechtenstein (LIE) to visa_free: confirmed as 'No necesita Visa' for ordinary passports on official table. Was missing from prior record.
CONFIRMED Albania (ALB) requires a visa for ordinary passports ('Necesita Visa (5)') - correctly not in visa_free list.
CONFIRMED China (CHN) conditional exemption issuers are US, Canada, UK, EU only - Australia is NOT a qualifying issuer. No change needed to existing conditional_access entries.
CONFIRMED Dominican Republic (DOM) credential condition: valid US or UK visa with at least 6 months remaining validity. No other issuers. Existing record correct.
CONFIRMED no e-visa or visa-on-arrival system exists for Uruguay as of validation date.
Note from 2026-06-27 validation ('Albania ordinary passport requires visa') was itself incorrect - 2026-06-29 re-fetch of live official page confirms Albania IS visa-free; gap note corrected.
2026-06-27 re-validation against official gub.uy/ministerio-interior/comunicacion/publicaciones/regimen-visas-admision (page dated 19/08/2025).

ADDED Armenia (ARM) to visa_free: official table lists 'Armenia' as 'No necesita Visa' for all passport types - was missing from prior record.
ADDED Macao SAR to visa_free: official table lists 'Macao RAE' as 'No necesita Visa (11)' per Note 11 - covers ordinary passports and Macao travel documents. Was missing from prior record.
ADDED Russia (RUS) to visa_free: official table lists 'Rusia' as 'No necesita Visa (6)' - visa-free for tourism only; non-tourism requires prior consultation. Was missing from prior record.
UPDATED Guyana (GUY) notes: official table shows 'No necesita Visa (6)' - Note 6 condition applies (prior consultation for non-tourism). Prior record had no condition note - corrected.

CORRECTED Andorra (AND) notes: official table shows ordinary passport = 'No necesita Visa', diplomatic = 'No necesita Visa', service = 'Necesita Visa'. Prior record incorrectly stated both diplomatic and service passports require a visa - diplomatic passport is actually visa-free.
CONFIRMED Philippines (PHL) ordinary passport = 'Necesita Visa (5)' - confirmed it was erroneously in visa_free in prior record.
CONFIRMED Guyana (GUY) = 'No necesita Visa (6)' - Note 6 condition now reflected in notes field.
2026-06-29 re-validation against official gub.uy/ministerio-interior/comunicacion/publicaciones/regimen-visas-admision.

ADDED Seychelles (SYC) to visa_free: official table lists 'Seychelles' as 'No necesita Visa' for ordinary passport - was missing from prior record.

ADDED Sovereign Military Order of Malta (SMOM) to visa_free: official table lists 'Malta Soberana Orden' as 'No necesita Visa' for ordinary passport - was missing from prior record; iso3=null as SMOM is not a UN member state.
CONFIRMED Belarus (BLR) ordinary passport = 'Necesita Visa (5)' - correctly NOT in visa_free; only diplomatic/service passports are visa-free via conditional_access.
CONFIRMED Fiji (FJI) ordinary passport = 'Necesita Visa (5)' - correctly NOT in visa_free.

CONFIRMED Dominica (DMA) = 'No necesita Visa (6)' - correctly in visa_free with Note 6 condition.
CONFIRMED Latvia (LVA) = 'No necesita Visa (6)' - correctly in visa_free with Note 6 condition.
CONFIRMED South Korea (KOR) = 'No necesita Visa (6)' - correctly in visa_free with Note 6 condition.
NOTE: Prior 2026-06-27 validation note stating 'CONFIRMED Albania (ALB) requires a visa (Necesita Visa (5))' was INCORRECT - the live official page shows Albania as visa-free. Corrected in this run.
gaps: The official Régimen de Visas de Admisión page (gub.uy/ministerio-interior/comunicacion/publicaciones/regimen-visas-admision) is in Spanish only; full enumeration of all ~140+ visa-required nationalities not captured - only visa-free countries were extracted from the official source.
Ireland and Austria appear on the official EU exemption list - confirmed visa-free consistent with EU-member enumeration; individually confirmed in official table text.
Dominican Republic conditional visa-free status (requires valid US or UK visa with 6 months) confirmed; may change with policy updates.
No minimum investment amounts found for any Uruguay residence program on official sources - Uruguay does not have an investment-threshold RBI (golden visa) program as of this research.
Uruguay has no official Citizenship-by-Investment program - confirmed via multiple official sources.
Taiwan (Chinese Taipei) - listed as 'Necesita Visa (8)' on official table as of 19/08/2025; not a visa-free entry.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder entry rule: No official Uruguayan source found granting OCI cardholders any special entry privilege beyond their underlying nationality passport.

Diplomatic/service passport bilateral agreement dates not individually confirmed from official text - list of countries sourced from official visa regime table but agreement signing dates not captured.

Andorra service passport requirement confirmed: official table shows ordinary = visa-free, diplomatic = visa-free, service = Necesita Visa. The conditional_access section does not have an entry for Andorra diplomatic (visa-free) since ordinary passport holders are already in visa_free. A conditional_access entry for Andorra service (visa required) is not warranted in this schema.

China diplomatic passport max-stay limit (30 days) sourced from cross-reference; official gub.uy page confirms diplomatic exemption but did not state explicit 30-day limit in scraped text - treat with caution.

Note 6 condition (Dominica, Guyana, Latvia, Russia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, South Korea): the exact 'prior consultation' process and authority (embassy, consulate, or DNM) is not specified in the official table.
Complete diplomatic/official passport visa-free exemption list may include additional countries not captured in this record.

Macao SAR (Note 11) - ordinary passport and travel documents confirmed visa-free; unclear whether non-standard Macao travel documents (e.g., laissez-passer) are covered beyond the standard RAE passport.
Sovereign Military Order of Malta (SMOM) added as visa-free; SMOM passport count and exact issuing criteria not confirmed from Uruguay official source.
Seychelles (SYC) confirmed visa-free; whether Note 6 conditions apply has not been explicitly checked - current record assumes unconditional 90-day entry.
Albania (ALB) confirmed visa-free; max stay and any note conditions not explicitly stated - current record assumes standard 90-day unconditional entry based on 'No necesita Visa' without footnote.

outbound_official.notes: No official Uruguay government outbound visa-list page located. Uruguay's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MRREE, gub.uy) does not publish a comprehensive country-by-country outbound visa-free list for Uruguayan ordinary passport holders. The closest official page (recomendaciones-generales-en-caso-de-viajar-al-exterior, dated 2019-02-18) only broadly states that Uruguayan citizens can travel to MERCOSUR member countries (Brazil, Paraguay, Argentina, Bolivia, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Venezuela) with a national ID card, and to Schengen area countries without a visa for up to 90 days in 180. The US requires a B1/B2 visa. Citizens are directed to contact destination country embassies for current requirements. The old MRREE domain (mrree.gub.uy) is no longer accessible. Rely on destination-side data for a complete picture.

visa_types.name: Admisión Temporaria Sin Visa (ATSV) / Visa de Turismo
visa_types.purpose: Entry for recreation, rest, or leisure purposes without conducting paid work. For nationalities requiring a visa, a Tourism Visa (Visa de Turismo) is issued by Uruguayan consulates.
visa_types.notes: Visa-free nationals receive 90 days automatically on entry, extendable once for additional 90 days. Nationals requiring a consular visa must apply at a Uruguayan embassy or consulate; the visa must be used within 180 days of issuance. Multiple entries permitted per Decree 356/018 Article 3. Governed by Migration Law 18.250 and Decree 356/018.

visa_types.name: Visa de Negocios
visa_types.purpose: For investment and commercial exchange activities with Uruguayan companies or public organisms. Does not permit employment or residence.
visa_types.notes: Renewable for an additional 90 days. Multiple entries permitted per Decree 356/018 Article 3. Must be applied for at a Uruguayan embassy or consulate. Governed by Decree 356/018.

visa_types.name: Visa de Trabajo
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who will develop labor activities in Uruguay. Requires initiating residency proceedings within 30 days of entry.
visa_types.notes: Duration depends on the migrant's residency subcategory (temporary: 6 months to 2 years; permanent: indefinite). Applicant must process legal residency within 30 days of entry. Applied for at Uruguayan consulates. Governed by Decree 356/018.

visa_types.name: Visa de Estudio
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals pursuing studies, scholarships, or internships at officially recognized Uruguayan educational institutions.
visa_types.notes: Duration regulated by temporary residency terms (minimum 6 months, maximum 2 years, renewable). Must process temporary residency within 30 days of entry. Proof of enrollment and financial support required. Applied for at Uruguayan consulates. Governed by Decree 356/018 and Migration Law 18.250 Art. 34.

visa_types.name: Visa de Reunificación Familiar
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals seeking to reunite with a family member who holds permanent legal residency in Uruguay.
visa_types.notes: Duration matches the sponsoring family member's residency period. Applicant must process residency within 30 days of entry. Applied for at Uruguayan consulates. Governed by Decree 356/018.

visa_types.name: Visa Humanitaria y de Urgencia
visa_types.purpose: For medical treatment, participation in judicial proceedings, or attendance due to family emergencies such as critical illness or death of a family member.
visa_types.notes: Duration tied to the specific humanitarian necessity. If the stay will exceed the visa period, applicant must apply for residency within 30 days. Applied for at Uruguayan consulates. Also covers judicial-related attendance. Governed by Decree 356/018.

visa_types.name: Visa de Congresos, Convenciones y Seminarios
visa_types.purpose: For attending national or international events of public interest such as congresses, conventions, seminars, and similar events.
visa_types.notes: Renewable for an additional 90 days. Multiple entries permitted per Decree 356/018 Article 3. Applied for at Uruguayan consulates. Governed by Decree 356/018.

visa_types.name: Visa Diplomática / Oficial / de Servicio
visa_types.purpose: For holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports representing their governments in Uruguay. Provides exemption from standard migration regulations.
visa_types.notes: Diplomatic personnel are exempt from standard entry/exit regulations under Migration Law 18.250 Art. 5; authorities merely verify documentation. Many nationalities that require visas on ordinary passports are visa-free on diplomatic/official/service passports via bilateral agreements. The Régimen de Visas de Admisión table lists specific exemptions by country and passport type.

visa_types.name: Residencia Legal Temporaria (Temporary Legal Residency)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals settling in Uruguay for work, study, religious activities, or other legally recognized purposes for stays of more than 6 months and up to 2 years.
visa_types.notes: Fee: 557.30 UI (Unidades Indexadas, adjusted semiannually). Additional: 55.70 UI for identity card; 225.60 UI per re-entry permit. Renewable for equal periods. Requires criminal background certificate, health card, vaccination certificate, proof of activity (work letter, enrollment, or religious affiliation). Online initial submission then in-person at National Migration Directorate (Mercedes 1004, Montevideo). Path to citizenship after 3 years of accumulated legal residency.

visa_types.name: Residencia Legal Permanente (Permanent Legal Residency)
visa_types.purpose: For any foreign national intending to reside permanently in Uruguay, regardless of nationality.
visa_types.notes: Fee: 557.30 UI. Additional: 55.70 UI for identity card; 225.60 UI per re-entry permit. Brazilian and Paraguayan nationals exempt from fees. Requires valid ID/passport, criminal background certificate, health card, vaccination certificate, and proof of income. Foreign documents must be apostilled or legalized and translated to Spanish (Brazilian documents exempt). Path to citizenship after 3 years of legal residency.

visa_types.name: Residencia Permanente Mercosur
visa_types.purpose: Permanent residency for nationals of Mercosur member and associate states (Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Bolivia, Paraguay, Peru, Ecuador, Colombia, Venezuela, Suriname, Guyana) and foreign family members of Uruguayan nationals.
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals born or naturalized in Mercosur member/associate countries and foreign family members of Uruguayan citizens (children, spouses, common-law partners, siblings). Path to citizenship after 3 years of legal residency. Fee structure similar to standard permanent residency.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporaria Mercosur
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for Mercosur and associated country nationals with at least 5 years of citizenship, allowing a 2-year stay renewable once.
visa_types.notes: Duration: 2 years, renewable once for additional 2 years. Renewal must be requested at least 90 days before expiration. Available to nationals of Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, Venezuela, Bolivia, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Guyana, and Suriname with 5+ years of citizenship. Path to citizenship after 3 years of legal residency.

visa_types.name: Permiso TID-Nómades (Digital Nomad Permit)
visa_types.purpose: For self-employed foreign nationals or those employed by companies outside Uruguay who wish to live and work remotely from Uruguay.
visa_types.notes: Established under Decree 238/022. Duration: 6 months, renewable once for additional 6 months (12 months total). Must first enter Uruguay as a regular tourist. Requirements: online application form, affidavit of financial self-sufficiency, clean criminal record (for extension), Uruguayan vaccination certificate. No minimum income threshold specified on official page. Can transition to temporary or permanent residency. Contact: dnm-hip@minterior.gub.uy.

visa_types.name: Documento Especial Fronterizo (Special Border Document)
visa_types.purpose: For Brazilian nationals residing in designated Uruguayan-Brazilian border cities, enabling work, study, or retirement/pension activities in Uruguay.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 5 years, renewable. Exclusively for Brazilian nationals living in designated border cities. Includes coverage for retirees and pensioners. Applied for at the National Migration Directorate.
